title: Switchboard Operators, Including Answering Service
description: Operate telephone business systems equipment or switchboards to relay incoming, outgoing, and interoffice calls. May supply information to callers and record messages.

tasks.text: Operate communication systems, such as telephone, switchboard, intercom, two-way radio, or public address.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer incoming calls, greeting callers, providing information, transferring calls or taking messages as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Greet visitors, log them in and out of the facility, assign them security badges, and contact employee escorts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor emergency and code alarms, make emergency announcements, or route emergency calls to the appropriate location.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Record messages, suggesting rewording for clarity or conciseness.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Page individuals to inform them of telephone calls, using paging or interoffice communication equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Relay or route written or verbal messages.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform various cash handling tasks, such as collecting payments, making bank deposits, or managing petty cash.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Place telephone calls or arrange conference calls as instructed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Process incoming or outgoing mail, packages, or deliveries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform various data entry or word processing tasks, such as updating phone directories, typing or proofreading documents, or creating schedules.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Perform administrative tasks, such as accepting orders, scheduling appointments or meeting rooms, or sending and receiving faxes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor alarm systems to ensure that secure conditions are maintained.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Contact security staff members when necessary, using radio-telephones.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer simple questions about clients' businesses, using reference files.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stamp messages with time and date and file them appropriately.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep records of calls placed and charges incurred.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Place orders, such as for equipment, supplies, or catering for meetings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
